purpose: Graph-RAG memory system using the Book of Luis concept graph (690 nodes, 1013 edges). Implements the Pico→Nano→Micro→Macro compression ladder and BRAID session memory (Was/Wasn't/Can Be).
motive: Solve the context collapse problem: agents accumulate session history until the context window fills. This replaces full history with a ~400-token compressed semantic territory map, relevant to each new task.
data_produced: BRAID entries (Was/Wasn't/Can Be per session, ~40 tokens each)
Graph-RAG context block (subgraph + path chains, ~200 tokens)
Combined session-start context (~400 tokens total, bounded regardless of history)

layers.pico: keyword seed search → top-k nodes by TF-IDF overlap + tier boost
layers.nano: BFS traversal via typed edges (proves_in > enables > implements > depends_on)
layers.micro: rerank by edge-type priority × traversal score decay
layers.macro: DATASPACE compression → Was/Wasn't/Can Be BRAID notation
